reasoning: On July 24, the Polymarket probability for 'No Change' stood around 42%. After the July 30 FOMC meeting (where rates were held steady) the odds for September remained uncertain. However, the August 1 jobs report completely changed the outlook: it revealed a massive miss (only 73k jobs added) alongside deep downward revisions to May and June totaling 258,000 lost jobs. This prompted an extremely rare public dissent from two Fed Governors (Waller and Bowman), who stated they wished they had cut rates in July. Analysts immediately upgraded the odds of a September cut to 75% via CME FedWatch. Given the overwhelming downward macro shift, the open dissent pushing for an immediate cut, and calls for potentially 50 basis point cuts to catch up, the probability of the Fed doing nothing in September is now very low, ~20-25%.
